threadWorkSequence.summary: Review classified OFARM as directionally right but organizationally and executably risky; main gaps were reference resolution, code-binding currentness, alias pinning, temporal conformance, and carrier-reference duplication.
Converted reviewer findings into implementation-facing risk language.
Defined Phases 0 through 6 and Deep Research need for external code-binding currentness.
Closed Phases 0 through 3 and staged Phase 4 research.
Selected Belgium/Phytoweb as narrow external currentness profile source and supplied fail-closed rules.
Closed Phases 4 and 5 at package-local conformance level.
Prepared final self-contained downloadable package with preserved source input and final completion metadata.
